title: install: make commanbds copy-pasteable
body: make the commands copy-pasteble: double click on a line and copy

comments.body: @glensc thanks for the PR.  We keep going back/forth on this type of change across the container projects.  Some people like the ease of the triple-click for cut/pasting, others like to see the command prompt to differentiate between commands that need to be run as root or as a rootless user.  I fall in the camp of preferring the command prompt to be shown.  If others would prefer the cut/paste ease, then the changes you're proposing LGTM.
if this were shown on a custom homepage, one could style the `#` vs `$` via CSS presentation layer
@rhatdan these docs are on GitHub. we have no control of their md->html rendering process.

Ok, I thought you knew something I did not know.

@TomSweeneyRedHat Instead of prefixing commands with `$` or `#` we could just use the command and add sudo when the command needs to be run as root.

That would help user distinguish the two use cases, and would work in cut and paste situations.
```
podman run -d -p 8080:80 nginx
```
vs
```
sudo podman run -d -p 80:80 nginx
```

I like the `sudo` distinction!

altho, i believe really most of the people run commands as `root` anyway :)

@rhatdan ack.  I'm with @glensc on this one, I'm not much for sudo and pref the command prompt symbols still.  But, you're right, distinguishing with sudo where needed would work too.

@glensc looks like you need a rebase on this one.

rebasing, but the allow edits is on, so maintainers can rebase themselves if they turned on the linear merge option and making contributions difficult requiring to rebase after each merge

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/199095/93978951-61b5bf00-fd85-11ea-8865-1703432bcc45.png)
